name: Pico del Terril and Peñón de Algámitas Circular
name_original: Subida a El Terril — circular desde Algámitas
description: Hike the difficult 11.8-mile Ascent to El Terril circular route from Algamitas for panoramic views from Seville's highest peak.
region_intro: Hiking around Algámitas offers access to the Sierra Sur of Seville, characterized by dramatic calcareous rock formations and Mediterranean vegetation. The region features prominent peaks such as El Terril, the highest point in Seville at 1,128 meters, and the Peñón de Algámitas, reaching 1,121 meters. The landscape includes holm oaks, cork oaks, and wild olive trees, with the Corbones River shaping parts of the terrain. Best hiking trails around Algámitas The most popular hiking route is Peñón de Algámitas Loop Trail, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 7 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views over the surrounding olive groves. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico del Terril and Peñón de Algámitas Circular, a difficult 11.8 miles (19.0 km) path. This strenuous route ascends to El Terril, providing extensive vistas from Seville's highest peak. Local hikers also love the Views of Algámitas – Peñón de Algámitas loop from Restaurante Mirador, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the Peñón, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Algámitas is defined by prominent peaks, calcareous rock formations, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Algámitas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Algámitas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find in Algámitas?
A: Algámitas offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from moderate paths to challenging ascents. The landscape is characterized by dramatic calcareous rock formations, prominent peaks like El Terril and Peñón de Algámitas, and Mediterranean forests with holm oaks and wild olive trees. There are 13 routes available, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Algámitas?
A: Yes, Algámitas has at least one easy trail. While many routes involve significant elevation, there are options for those seeking a less strenuous experience. For example, the region's proximity to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1043791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; offers a flatter, historical path, though specific easy routes within Algámitas itself might be limited to shorter, less steep sections.

Q: Which trails offer the best panoramic views in Algámitas?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider trails that ascend the region's highest peaks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009613445/pico-del-terril-and-penon-de-algamitas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Terril and Peñón de Algámitas Circular&lt;/a&gt; route, though strenuous, provides extensive vistas from El Terril, Seville's highest point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009626125/penon-de-algamitas-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Algámitas Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; also offers rewarding views over the surrounding olive groves.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Algámitas?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009626125/penon-de-algamitas-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Algámitas Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 5.5 km circular option. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009613445/pico-del-terril-and-penon-de-algamitas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Terril and Peñón de Algámitas Circular&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 19 km route that takes you to both prominent peaks. For a moderate option, try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14590394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Algámitas – Peñón de Algámitas loop from Restaurante Mirador&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while hiking in Algámitas?
A: Hikers in Algámitas can explore impressive natural features such as the prominent peaks of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4048502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Terril&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Algámitas&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features typical Mediterranean vegetation, including holm oaks and cork oaks, and is shaped by the Corbones River. Historically, the Ruta de los Bandoleros explores natural caves once used by outlaws.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Algámitas?
A: The best time to hike in Algámitas is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural vegetation, especially the 'Bosque de las Hadas' (Fairy Forest), is particularly vibrant with blooming plants and flowers in spring. Summers can be very hot, making longer hikes challenging.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Algámitas?
A: While many trails in Algámitas involve significant elevation and can be challenging, shorter, less steep sections or the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1043791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; offer more accessible options for families. The Via Verde, following an old railway line, provides a relatively flat and scenic path suitable for both walking and cycling.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Algámitas?
A: The trails in Algámitas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the challenging ascents, the rewarding panoramic views from the peaks, and the diverse Mediterranean landscapes.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty hikes in Algámitas?
A: Yes, Algámitas offers several moderate difficulty hikes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14590394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Algámitas – Peñón de Algámitas loop from Restaurante Mirador&lt;/a&gt; is a 5 km trail with varied terrain. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14590444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Algámitas – Pico del Terril loop from Restaurante Mirador&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 8.9 km.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Algámitas?
A: While specific parking information for every trailhead isn't detailed, Algámitas is a village focused on rural tourism. It's generally possible to find parking in or near the village, especially around popular starting points like Restaurante Mirador, which is mentioned as a starting point for some loops. We recommend checking local signage upon arrival.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The Mediterranean forests and rocky outcrops of Algámitas are home to various wildlife. While specific sightings depend on luck and time of day, you might encounter birds of prey soaring above the peaks, as well as smaller mammals and reptiles typical of the Sierra Sur region. The diverse vegetation also supports a rich insect life.
tips: Collado Sierra de Tablón is a highlight on the circular route that includes Sierra de Tablón and El Terril, near Algámitas, in the province of Seville. This route is known for its difficulty, requiring good physical fitness and experience in high mountains.

Pico del Terril is the highest altitude in the Spanish province of Seville in Andalusia, at 1,129 meters above sea level. It is located on the border line between the terms of the towns of Algámitas and Pruna, on the summit of the hill known as "El Monigote" which is the highest in the Sierra del Tablón.

A small town located in the Sierra del Tablón. It is at the foot of El Peñón de Algámitas, the true symbol of this municipality. El Peñón is the highest point in the province of Seville (with 1121 masl).

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from the village of Algámitas. Parking is generally available within the village. The trailhead itself can be a bit inconspicuous, sometimes marked by anonymous wooden signs and overgrown vegetation, so pay close attention to your navigation.

faqs.question: Is the Ascent to El Terril trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is suitable for hiking with dogs year-round, as there are no pine processionary caterpillars in the area. However, please keep your dog controlled, especially around farm animals like goats and sheep, which you may encounter.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike El Terril?
faqs.answer: Spring is an excellent time to visit, as the hills are adorned with vibrant wildflowers. However, due to the open landscape and lack of shade in upper sections, it's advisable to avoid the hottest parts of summer. Autumn and even mild winter days can also be pleasant.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to hike in the Sierra del Tablón?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for hiking this route in the Sierra del Tablón.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ascent to El Terril circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied and challenging. It begins on a forest track through olive groves, transitioning to a steep, sometimes overgrown path. As you ascend, it becomes very stony and scrubby, with the final push to the summit being particularly steep (12% to 23% inclination). The descent can also be challenging, especially if wet.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: From the summit of Pico del Terril, you'll be rewarded with breathtaking 360-degree panoramic views of three provinces: Seville, Cadiz, and Malaga. You can also see the Sierra Nevada and the village of Algámitas below. The Sierra de Tablón Pass is another significant point along the route.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and exposed sections, it's crucial to carry plenty of water, as natural sources are scarce. Sturdy hiking shoes with good grip are essential. Hiking poles can be very helpful, especially on steep and overgrown sections. Dressing in layers is recommended to adapt to changing weather conditions, as the open landscape offers little shelter from sun or wind.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with official trails such as 'Subida a El Terril' and 'CIMA SV04] Peñón de Algámitas'. It is also part of the Jacobea de la Frontera, adding a historical and cultural dimension to the hike.

faqs.question: Is this a suitable hike for beginners or families with young children?
faqs.answer: No, this is a difficult hike requiring very good physical fitness and sure-footedness. The substantial elevation gain, steep sections, and varied terrain make it unsuitable for beginners or families with young children. It's best for experienced hikers.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for this route?
faqs.answer: The initial section from Algámitas can be steep and overgrown, so hiking poles are particularly useful here for support and moving vegetation. The upper sections offer little shade, so plan for sun exposure. The descent can be tricky, especially if wet. Always carry more water than you think you'll need, as there are no reliable water sources on the trail.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The lower slopes feature dense vegetation including holm oak, wild olive trees, and carob trees. As you gain elevation, this gives way to specialized rock-dwelling species. While not explicitly detailed, the area is part of a natural sierra, so you might spot various birds and small mammals. Keep an eye out for farm animals like goats and sheep.
